title: atriplex pentandra subsp. confinis
intro: Atriplex pentandra subsp. confinis, also known as inland saltbush, is a shrub-like plant native to the dry regions of Australia. Traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine, inland saltbush has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of wounds. Inland saltbush can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and salves, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
